must_detect.matched_text:
Confidential incident report prepared for Rimal Horizon Energy PJSC Board Risk Committee; jurisdiction: UAE PDPL, SCA, ADX, ADGM/DIFC coordination in scope.
On 2026-05-29, a credential-phishing event led to unauthorized access to a limited HR metadata index and a deal subfolder within the sustainability-linked sukuk VDR for a prospective ADX listing; the information is non-public and market-sensitive.
The exposed deal notes included draft coupon range 4.9–5.2%, an indicative Q4 window, and preliminary underwriter shortlists (Falaj Securities LLC; Pearl Dunes Capital Ltd.), none of which has been disclosed to the market.
Mr. Kareem Al‑Hamdan (Incident Response Analyst, Rimal Horizon Energy PJSC, Abu Dhabi HQ), DOB 1991-04-17 (age 33), work email k.alhamdan@rimhorizon.ae, VPN account rhamdan_uae with session SID-ABDX-7732, accessing via corporate laptop RH-LAP-0417, public IP 203.0.113.45, mobile IMEI IMEI-FIC-90210.
Log artifacts referencing his profile included Emirates ID ref EID-TEST-91A4-ABU-0417, internal HR card HRP-RH-2291, payroll ledger LA-ESG-00987, and event index i-ADX-124; trade licence for the issuer appears as TL-RH-100234-AD (masked).
Access was initiated from the Abu Dhabi HQ and during a brief visit to the Dubai project office; AuthAxis event AX-AD-55772 and NetSentra alert NS-ABU-441 correlate with the same session.
Separately, a log line reads "male, early 40s, warehouse foreman in Fujairah, smartphone model A12, alias foreman_02" with no names or linkable IDs; standing alone, such descriptors should not identify a natural person.
No scanned Emirates ID images or passport pages were exfiltrated; a family-origin field in an HR form ("Ras Al Qamar lineage") was toggled during access but the value did not leave the system.
Pearl Gate Capital Limited (DIFC Branch) was notified as transaction advisor; SCA Market Surveillance has been pre-briefed, and an ADGM FSRA courtesy notice was logged due to ADGM-hosted data room infrastructure.
ESG registers and emissions baselines were unaffected; however, CSR sub-ledger CSR-ACCT-774 recorded a failed login from 198.51.100.31 which appears unrelated and is being tracked separately.
Contact: incidents@rimhorizon.ae or +971-600-555-021 (Incident Desk at Sand Dhow Response FZ-LLC, retained cyber advisor).
Under UAE PDPL, the weak identifiers above form PD under certain combinations; this report avoids full Emirates ID numbers and bank IBANs by design.
The draft term sheet for Rimal Horizon Energy PJSC and internal revenue guidance for Q3 FY2026 remain non-public; onward sharing may constitute market abuse under SCA and exchange rules.

must_not_detect.reason: Standalone weak descriptor; not identifying on its own

uncertain.reason: Sensitive family/ethnic origin field relevant under PDPL special categories but not explicitly listed in allowed rules
Potentially a trackable internal event ID; not clearly mapped to internal_session_id rule
Alert identifier may indirectly identify a user/session; not clearly covered by listed rules
Ledger/account-like reference; may be linkable but not clearly covered by allowed categories
Payroll ledger reference may be linkable yet no exact rule match provided
Internal index that could link to an event/user; not clearly covered by allowed rules
Asset tag functions like a device identifier but may not be a true serial number as per rule intent
Login username is identifying but not explicitly mapped to a listed PII rule
Internal project label might act like a codename but does not match the 'Project <Name>' pattern
Internal issuer code could be sensitive in context but not mapped to any allowed rule
